title: Selenipedium × giganteum
taxonomy: Selenipedium × giganteum is a hybrid species of orchid, belonging to the family Orchidaceae. It is a cross between two species of Selenipedium, specifically Selenipedium palmifolium and Selenipedium ishpinguense. This hybrid orchid is known for its large flowers and is native to South America. The classification of Selenipedium × giganteum as a hybrid species is due to its unique characteristics and genetic makeup. It is often cultivated and bred in orchid gardens for its striking appearance.

intro: Selenipedium × giganteum is a hybrid orchid species, also known as the giant bird's nest orchid, which is native to tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Selenipedium × giganteum include alkaloids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
